_comment: WORKED EXAMPLE (replace with your own field): this file targets People/HR roles for an EU (Spain-home) candidate purely as a filled-in illustration. Copy it to scraper_config.json, then replace role_keywords / exclude_keywords / override_keywords / jobicy_industry / remotive_category / himalayas_queries / eligible_regions / home_country with the terms and geography of YOUR field (engineering, sales, design, finance, etc.). Keys prefixed with '_' are ignored by the loader. With NO scraper_config.json present, the scraper falls back to a generic worldwide-remote, match-all search.
_role_keywords: Title must contain at least one of these (case-insensitive substring). Empty list = match ALL titles.
_exclude_keywords: Drop a title if it contains any of these (avoids off-target roles).
_override_keywords: Allowlist that RESCUES an otherwise-excluded title if it also matches one of these (e.g. 'People Systems Engineer').
_jobicy_industry: Jobicy API industry filter (https://jobicy.com/jobs-rss-feed#api). 'hr' = Human Resources. Empty = no industry filter.
_remotive_category: Remotive API category slug. Empty = no category filter.
_himalayas_queries: Search queries run against the Himalayas API (one request each).
_eligible_regions: Which geo tiers to keep. Recognized values: 'worldwide', a 'home_country' name (e.g. 'spain'), 'europe'/'eu'/'emea', 'us'/'united states'. classify_geo maps each posting to worldwide | home | eu | us_only | other; a posting is kept if its tier is eligible. For a US candidate, use e.g. ["worldwide", "us"].
_home_country: Your home country (lowercase). Postings naming it are tiered 'home' and ranked just after worldwide. Empty = no home tier.
_past_company_slugs: Former employers to skip by default, as lowercase underscore-separated name slugs (e.g. ['acme_corp', 'globex']). Re-include any for a single run with --boomerang acme_corp.
